tone_examples: Quick check — your weekday 7-9pm slot has been at 90%+ capacity all month
Footfall pattern: April drop-off is normal; bookings recover by 2nd week May

digest.title: Post-Jan resolution window closing — last 2 weeks of high trial-walk-ins
digest.summary: Gym trial walk-ins spike Jan 1-15, taper through Mar; April-June hits the lowest acquisition window of the year. Most gyms over-spend on ads now; underspend in October pre-holiday window.
digest.actionable: Pause acquisition spend in May; double down Sept-Oct

digest.title: Personal Training inquiries +38% YoY in 30-50 corporate cohort
digest.summary: Driven by health-check-up findings (rising HbA1c, BP, cholesterol). Demand is for 2x/week PT, not full-program. ₹3,000-5,000/month sweet spot.
digest.actionable: Package a '2x/week PT @ ₹3,499' option separate from full membership

digest.title: Boutique yoga/pilates studios opening fast in metro neighbourhoods
digest.summary: Reformer pilates studios at ₹6,000-8,000/month membership are pulling 30-45 women from traditional gyms. Don't compete on price; compete on coach quality + community.
digest.actionable: Add one yoga or pilates class to your weekly schedule if you don't have one

digest.title: ICMR creatine supplementation safety bulletin — adolescent guidance
digest.summary: Creatine monohydrate at 3-5g/day deemed safe for healthy adults; cautioned against under-18 use without medical supervision. Reinforces what most coaches already practice.
digest.actionable: Update your store / counter recommendations; flag under-18 customers

digest.title: Schedule density study — peak slots underutilized in mornings
digest.summary: Across 200+ partner gyms, 6-8am weekday slots run at 60% capacity vs 90%+ for 6-9pm. Adding a 7am class typically doesn't cannibalize evenings.
digest.actionable: Consider one new 7am class — yoga or HIIT pulls best in that slot

patient_content_library.title: First 30 days at the gym — what actually changes
patient_content_library.body: Week 1-2: nervous-system adaptation, lifts feel easier (not muscle gain — coordination). Week 3-4: small visible changes, sleep improves. Real strength/aesthetic changes start at week 8-12. Most people quit at week 3 expecting visible change. Trust the process.

patient_content_library.title: PT or solo? A 5-question test
patient_content_library.body: Solo if: you've trained before, you can read program structure, you don't get injured easily. PT if: you're new, recovering from injury, training for a specific event, or have inconsistent form. Hybrid (PT 1-2x/month + solo): the smartest middle path for most members.

patient_content_library.title: How much protein do you actually need?
patient_content_library.body: Sedentary adults: 0.8g/kg body weight. Active adults: 1.2-1.6g/kg. Strength training: 1.6-2.2g/kg. For a 70kg active person, that's 84-112g/day — roughly 4 fistful-portions of protein. Indian-veg diets often fall short; supplement with paneer, dal, tofu before adding powders.

seasonal_beats.note: resolution surge — trial walk-ins 4x baseline; convert window
lowest acquisition window — focus on retention, not acquisition
wedding-prep + festival window — repeat clients return to shape up
holiday slowdown — class density drops 25%; right time to renovate or pilot new programs
